LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E) AND (F).) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STRICT PRODUCT LIABILITY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT RENTED NEW SEGWAYS TO ITS CUSTOMERS. (SEE WILKINSON V. HICKS (1981) 126 CAL.APP.3D 515, 519; ARRIAGA V. CITICAPITAL COMMERCIAL CORP. (2008) 167 CAL.APP.4TH 1527, 1541.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE KNOWLEDGE OF FALSITY OR INTENT TO DEFRAUD. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED BECAUSE THE CONTRACT AND WAIVER AGREEMENT IS NOT ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT AND THEREFORE THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNCERTAIN. (ALAMEDA COUNTY TITLE INS. CO. V. PANELLA (1933) 218 CAL. 510, 513; FPI DEVELOPMENT, INC. V. NAKASHIMA (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 367, 383.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEMURRER, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD A BASIS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHARLES KEES, AND CHARLES KEES DBA SEGWAY SAN FRANCISCO BAY'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Monetary Damages as to punitive damages is granted. Plaintiffs' "conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement" to support their claim for punitive damages. (Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) General allegations that Defendant Segway, Inc. knowingly concealed the dangers of its product and did not provide a long enough training video, and that Defendant Charles Kees And Charles Kees Dba Segway San Francisco Bay's acted in a "despicable" manner by saying that the Segway was "100 percent safe" do not meet threshold requirements for punitive damages. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHARLES KEES, AND CHARLES KEES DBA SEGWAY SAN FRANCISCO BAY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to Plaintiff's cause of action for fraud is sustained. Plaintiffs are not persuasive in asserting that either defendant committed fraud. Plaintiffs' pleadings do not offer " 'facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.' " (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645, citing Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) Plaintiffs also fail to "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written", which is required for sustaining a claim of fraud against a corporate defendant. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) Plaintiffs have not indicated how they can cure their inadequate pleadings. Leave to amend is granted if Plaintiffs can in good faith plead the specifics required. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEVEN R. DISHAROON of Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman, LLP, Tel No. (925) 222-3421 appearing for Defendants Charles Kees and Charles Kees dba Segway San Francisco Bay;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHARLES KEES AND CHARLES KEES DBA SEGWAY SAN FRANCISCO BAY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the second cause of action for strict product liability, the demurrer is sustained because the complaint does not allege that Defendant rented new Segways to its customers. (See Wilkinson v. Hicks (1981) 126 Cal.App.3d 515, 519; Arriaga v. CitiCapital Commercial Corp. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1527, 1541.) As to the third cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained because the complaint does not adequately allege knowledge of falsity or intent to defraud. As to the fourth cause of action for fraud in the inducement, the demurrer is sustained because the contract and waiver agreement is not attached to the complaint and therefore the cause of action is uncertain. (Alameda County Title Ins. Co. v. Panella (1933) 218 Cal. 510, 513; FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 383.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
